task_completion.description: Configure Stellarium to the correct location and time
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the Stellarium location window showing 'Point Venus', 'Tahiti', or coordinates near 17.49° S, 149.49° W. Look for the date/time window showing the date 1769-06-03 and time 21:00 UTC (or equivalent local time).

task_completion.description: Apply the required display settings in Stellarium
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the Stellarium sky view, the sky must be black indicating the atmosphere is disabled. A circular coordinate grid (azimuthal grid) must be visible. Text labels for celestial bodies (like the Sun and Venus) must be turned on.

task_completion.description: Center the Sun and observe the Transit of Venus
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Sun should be centered and zoomed in on the screen. A small, distinct dark circle (the silhouette of Venus) must be visible crossing the bright disk of the Sun.

task_completion.description: Save the screenshot to the correct directory
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a file manager window showing an image file inside the '~/Pictures/stellarium' directory, a terminal command verifying its existence, or a Stellarium on-screen notification confirming the screenshot was saved.

task_completion.description: Create the exhibit placard text file on the Desktop
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a text editor (e.g., gedit, nano, mousepad) open with a file named 'transit_exhibit.txt' saved in the '~/Desktop' directory, or a terminal command showing the file was created there.

task_completion.description: Include all required historical and scientific facts in the placard
task_completion.visual_evidence: Read the contents of 'transit_exhibit.txt'. It must explicitly contain: the location ('Tahiti' or 'Point Venus'), the year ('1769'), the observer ('Cook'), and a mention of calculating the 'distance to the Sun' or 'astronomical unit'.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used the Stellarium application to render the scene
integrity.visual_evidence: Check that the Stellarium application interface is visible and was used to generate the view, rather than a web browser displaying a historical image or an image viewer showing a pre-downloaded picture.

integrity.description: Verify the agent did not download a pre-existing image of the transit
integrity.visual_evidence: Ensure there are no web browser windows open to Google Images, Wikipedia, or other sites where the agent might have downloaded a picture of the 1769 transit instead of simulating it.

integrity.description: Verify the agent genuinely composed the placard text
integrity.visual_evidence: Check that the text was typed or constructed in a text editor or via standard terminal echo/cat commands, rather than copying a pre-existing solution file.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Target: Sun. Event: Transit of Venus. Location: Point Venus, Tahiti (17.49°S, 149.49°W). Date: 1769-06-03. Time: 21:00:00 UTC. Required display flags: Atmosphere OFF, Azimuthal Grid ON, Planet Labels ON. Placard path: /home/ga/Desktop/transit_exhibit.txt. Screenshot directory: /home/ga/Pictures/stellarium.
